title: Water Crisis Hits Jackson
story: Imagine turning on your tap and nothing comes out! That's what happened in Jackson, Mississippi in 2022. The city's biggest water plant broke down on August 30, leaving about 150,000 people without safe water to drink or use. Schools and businesses had to close. It was a tough time, but the community came together to help each other through the crisis.
activity_question: What broke down in Jackson, Mississippi?
follow_up_question: How might you help if your town had a water crisis?

title: A Baseball Legend's First Swing
story: Crack! The sound of bat meeting ball echoed through Bennett Park in Detroit. A young player named Ty Cobb had just hit a double in his very first Major League Baseball game. It was August 30, 1905, and the Detroit Tigers were facing the New York Highlanders. Cobb's impressive debut helped his team win 5-3, launching a career that would make him a Baseball Hall of Fame legend.
activity_question: What type of hit did Ty Cobb get in his first MLB game?
follow_up_question: Can you name the team Ty Cobb played for in his debut?

title: Joe Louis Punches His Way to Victory
story: Pow! Bam! In 1937, Joe Louis showed why he was called the 'Brown Bomber.' On August 30, he faced Tommy Farr in a fierce boxing match. For 15 grueling rounds, the two fighters traded blows. In the end, Louis's powerful punches won the day. He kept his heavyweight boxing title, thrilling fans with his skill and determination.
activity_question: What was Joe Louis's nickname?
follow_up_question: Why do you think boxers often have nicknames?

title: A Storyteller's Magical Birthday
story: Imagine a world where stories come to life! Donald Bisset, the creator of whimsical tales and colorful illustrations, was born on August 30, 1910, in Brentford, England. This talented artist wore many hats - he wrote and illustrated children's books, including the delightful 'Upside Down Stories', and even acted in films like 'Battle of the Sexes'.
activity_question: What kind of stories did Donald Bisset create?
follow_up_question: Can you think of a fun story idea that could fit in an 'Upside Down' book?

title: Bouncing into Basketball History
story: William 'Pop' Gates dribbled his way into basketball legend. Born in Decatur, Alabama on August 30, 1917, he became a star guard and coach. Gates showed off his skills with the famous Harlem Globetrotters, amazing fans with his tricks and talent. His impact on the game earned him a spot in the Basketball Hall of Fame and the Globetrotters' 'Legends' Ring in 1995.
activity_question: What team was William 'Pop' Gates famous for playing with?
bonus_fact: The Harlem Globetrotters are known for mixing amazing basketball skills with comedy and entertainment.
